Steps.Name: DNS Resolution
Steps.Description: Verifies that DNS servers are configured and can resolve external hostnames.
Steps.UserFeedback: Checking DNS server configuration and resolution...

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Open Network Adapter Settings and set DNS to 8.8.8.8 (Primary) and 8.8.4.4 (Secondary)
2. Run 'ipconfig /flushdns' from an elevated command prompt
3. Run 'ipconfig /renew' to refresh your network lease

TestResultText.Warning: DNS may be partially functional. Some name resolutions could fail intermittently.
TestResultText.Failed: DNS servers are not configured or unable to resolve hostnames. Internet access may be broken.
TestResultText.Passed: DNS servers are responding and resolving external hostnames correctly.

Steps.Name: Default Gateway
Steps.Description: Confirms the default gateway is configured and reachable via ICMP ping.
Steps.UserFeedback: Pinging your default gateway to confirm network path...

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Run 'ipconfig /release' then 'ipconfig /renew' to request a new DHCP lease
2. Restart your router or managed switch
3. Verify the network cable is connected or WiFi is turned on

TestResultText.Warning: Gateway response was inconsistent. Network performance may be degraded.
TestResultText.Failed: Default gateway cannot be reached. You may be disconnected from the local network.
TestResultText.Passed: Default gateway is reachable. Layer-3 routing to the local network is healthy.

Steps.Name: Proxy Configuration
Steps.Description: Checks whether a WinHTTP proxy is configured, which may be required in corporate environments.

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. If a corporate proxy is required, configure it via Settings > Network and Internet > Proxy
2. Or run (as Admin): netsh winhttp set proxy proxy-server='proxyserver:port'
3. Contact IT support for the correct proxy address and port

TestResultText.Warning: No proxy is configured. This may be expected on home networks, but required in corporate environments.
TestResultText.Passed: A proxy server is configured. Corporate traffic should route correctly.

Steps.Name: Network Adapters
Steps.Description: Checks for at least one active physical network adapter.

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Open Device Manager (devmgmt.msc) and check for disabled or error-flagged adapters
2. Right-click the adapter and choose Enable or Update Driver
3. Verify the network cable is connected or the WiFi radio is enabled

TestResultText.Warning: Adapter state is indeterminate. Review Device Manager for details.
TestResultText.Failed: No active physical adapters found. The device may be disconnected or drivers may need attention.
TestResultText.Passed: One or more physical network adapters are active and ready.

Steps.Name: Firewall Status
Steps.Description: Verifies that at least one Windows Firewall profile is enabled.

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Open Windows Security > Firewall and Network Protection and enable for each profile
2. Or run (as Admin): Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -Enabled True
3. Contact IT if Group Policy is preventing the change

TestResultText.Warning: Windows Firewall appears to be disabled. This is a security risk; re-enable it if possible.
TestResultText.Failed: Firewall query failed. Review Windows Security for details.
TestResultText.Passed: Windows Firewall is active on at least one network profile.

Steps.Name: Internet Connectivity
Steps.Description: Pings Google's public DNS (8.8.8.8) to confirm external internet access.
Steps.UserFeedback: Testing external internet connectivity via ping...

ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Confirm the network cable is plugged in or WiFi is connected
2. Restart your modem and router (unplug for 30 seconds, then reconnect)
3. Run 'ipconfig /flushdns' then 'ipconfig /renew'
4. Contact your ISP if the issue persists after the above steps

TestResultText.Warning: Internet access is intermittent. Packets may be dropping or routing is unstable.
TestResultText.Failed: Cannot reach the public internet. Local network may be up but WAN connectivity is broken.
TestResultText.Passed: External internet access is confirmed. Traffic can reach the public internet.

Steps.Name: WiFi Signal Strength
Steps.Description: Reports wireless signal quality as a percentage and rates it Excellent / Good / Fair / Weak.
Steps.UserFeedback: Measuring wireless signal strength via netsh...

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Move closer to the wireless access point
2. Reduce physical obstructions (walls, furniture) between the device and AP
3. Switch to the 5 GHz band if available
4. Check for interference from other nearby wireless devices or appliances

TestResultText.Warning: WiFi signal is weak or undetermined. Consider moving closer to the access point.
TestResultText.Failed: WiFi signal check failed. The adapter may not be responding.
TestResultText.Passed: WiFi signal strength is being measured and reported successfully.

Steps.Name: VPN Status
Steps.Description: Checks whether any VPN connections are configured and if any are currently active.

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Open your VPN client and connect to the appropriate server
2. Verify your VPN credentials have not expired
3. Contact IT support for VPN server details or a credential reset

TestResultText.Warning: No active VPN connection detected. If VPN access is required for your work, connect before continuing.
TestResultText.Failed: VPN status query encountered an unexpected error.

Steps.Name: Internet Speed Test
Steps.Description: Downloads a small reference image and measures response time as a proxy for connection speed.

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Restart your modem and router to clear congestion
2. Close background applications that consume bandwidth (streaming, cloud sync)
3. Try connecting via Ethernet cable instead of WiFi for a more stable connection
ResolutionText.Failed:
1. Confirm internet connectivity first (ping 8.8.8.8)
2. Restart your modem and router
3. Check for bandwidth-heavy background applications or downloads
4. Contact your ISP if speeds remain consistently low

TestResultText.Warning: Connection is slow. Consider restarting networking equipment or switching to a wired connection.
TestResultText.Failed: Speed test could not complete. No internet access or the test endpoint is unreachable.
TestResultText.Passed: Internet response time is within normal range.

Steps.Name: VPN Speed (if connected)
Steps.Description: If a VPN is active, measures download latency through the VPN tunnel.
Steps.UserFeedback: Testing download latency through the VPN tunnel (if active)...

ResolutionText.Warning:
1. Disconnect and reconnect VPN to obtain a fresher server connection
2. Switch to a VPN server geographically closer to your location
3. Contact IT support if high latency persists across multiple servers

TestResultText.Warning: VPN is either not connected (step skipped) or latency is high. Reconnect or switch servers.
TestResultText.Passed: VPN tunnel latency is within acceptable range.
